nodes.name: Excavation & Shoring
nodes.desc: Bulk and detailed excavation, sheeting, shoring, and underpinning. Typical trades: equipment operators, general laborers. Often the first major activity on site — schedule and cost overruns here cascade through the project.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '31 23 16'; description matches: excavation, shoring, lagging, soldier piles

nodes.name: Backfill & Grading
nodes.desc: Structural fill, fine grading, compaction, and subgrade preparation. Equipment-heavy — dozers, loaders, compactors. Quality depends on compaction testing (typically third-party geotechnical).
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '31 23 23'; description matches: backfill, fill, compaction, grading

nodes.name: Dewatering
nodes.desc: Wellpoints, sump pumps, and groundwater control during construction. Triggered by water table conditions — not present on every project. Can become a major unplanned cost if unexpected.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '31 23 19'; description matches: dewatering, well points, sump

nodes.name: Erosion Control
nodes.desc: Temporary and permanent measures to prevent soil erosion and sediment runoff during construction, including silt fencing, erosion blankets, and hydroseeding.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '31 25 00'; description matches: erosion control, silt fence, SWPPP

nodes.name: Deep Foundations
nodes.desc: Driven piles, drilled shafts, caissons, micropiles, and helical piers. Requires specialized subcontractors and equipment. Production rates (piles per day) are a key schedule driver. Trades: pile drivers, equipment operators.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '31 62 00 / 03 30 00'; description matches: drilled piers, micropiles, augercast piles, caissons

nodes.name: Shallow Foundations
nodes.desc: Spread footings, strip footings, mat foundations, and pier pads. Concrete and formwork are the primary cost drivers. Trades: concrete workers, carpenters (formwork). Pour records track volume and placement.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '03 30 00'; description matches: spread footings, mat foundation, isolated footings

nodes.name: Foundation Walls
nodes.desc: Cast-in-place or precast foundation walls and retaining walls. Formwork complexity drives cost. Waterproofing is a critical follow-on trade. Trades: concrete workers, carpenters.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '03 30 00'; description matches: foundation walls, basement walls, retaining walls

nodes.name: Grade Beams
nodes.desc: Reinforced concrete beams connecting foundation elements at or below grade. Transfer loads between piles/piers. Trades: concrete workers, carpenters.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '03 30 00'; description matches: grade beams, pile caps

nodes.name: Slab on Grade
nodes.desc: Ground-supported concrete floor slabs with vapor barriers and joint sealants. Includes reinforcement (rebar, WWF, fiber). Quality depends on subgrade prep and finishing. Trades: concrete workers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '03 30 00'; description matches: slab on grade, SOG, ground floor slab

nodes.name: Below-Grade Waterproofing
nodes.desc: Foundation waterproofing membranes, dampproofing, underslab vapor barriers, and drainage boards. Failure leads to long-term moisture intrusion. Trades: specialty waterproofing applicators.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '07 10 00'; description matches: below-grade waterproofing, dampproofing, blindside waterproofing

nodes.name: Water Main
nodes.desc: Underground domestic and fire water supply piping from street to building. Requires coordination with municipality. Trades: plumbers/pipefitters, equipment operators.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '33 11 00'; description matches: water main, water service, water distribution

nodes.name: Sanitary Sewer
nodes.desc: Underground sanitary waste piping from building to main, with manholes and cleanouts. Gravity-driven — slope is critical. Trades: plumbers/pipefitters, equipment operators.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '33 30 00'; description matches: sanitary sewer, sewer line, sanitary main

nodes.name: Storm Drainage
nodes.desc: Underground storm water piping, catch basins, and retention/detention systems. Sized by hydrological analysis. Subject to local stormwater regulations. Trades: plumbers/pipefitters, equipment operators.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '33 40 00'; description matches: storm drainage, storm sewer, catch basin

nodes.name: Gas Service
nodes.desc: Underground gas piping from meter to building. Small scope on most projects but requires specialty inspection. Trades: plumbers/pipefitters.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '33 51 00'; description matches: gas service, natural gas main

nodes.name: Site Electrical
nodes.desc: Underground electrical and telecom conduit banks, manholes, and handholes. Coordinates with power company. Trades: electricians, equipment operators.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '33 70 00'; description matches: site electrical, site lighting power, exterior power distribution

nodes.name: Columns
nodes.desc: Steel or concrete vertical load-bearing members. Steel columns: W-shapes, HSS, pipe. Concrete columns: CIP with rebar cages, formwork. Trades: ironworkers (steel), concrete workers (concrete). Critical path activity.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '03 30 00 + 05 12 00'; description matches: columns, concrete columns, structural steel columns

nodes.name: Beams & Girders
nodes.desc: Steel or concrete horizontal spanning members. Steel: W-shapes, built-up sections. Concrete: CIP or precast. Camber and deflection are key design parameters. Trades: ironworkers, concrete workers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '03 30 00 + 05 12 00'; description matches: beams, girders, structural beams

nodes.name: Floor Slabs & Deck
nodes.desc: Concrete floor slabs on grade and elevated composite or concrete deck systems forming the horizontal structure of each level.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '03 30 00 + 05 31 00'; description matches: elevated slabs, structural slab, deck, composite deck, metal deck

nodes.name: Roof Structure
nodes.desc: Steel or concrete roof framing — long-span joists, trusses, and moment frames. Trades: ironworkers. Often governs crane duration and pick sequence.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '05 12 00 + 06 17 00'; description matches: roof framing, roof joists, roof trusses, glulam

nodes.name: Connections & Embeds
nodes.desc: Bolted and welded connections, embed plates, anchor bolts, and base plates. Connection design generates heavy RFI activity — a leading indicator of structural complexity. Trades: ironworkers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '05 50 00'; description matches: connections, embeds, anchors, baseplates

nodes.name: Stairs & Ramps
nodes.desc: Cast-in-place or steel stairs and structural ramps, with handrails. Both temporary and permanent stairs. Punch items frequently reference stairs and railings. Trades: ironworkers, concrete workers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '03 30 00 + 05 51 00'; description matches: stairs, ramps, stair stringers, metal stairs

nodes.name: Misc Metals
nodes.desc: Miscellaneous and ornamental metals including handrails, guardrails, bollards, ladders, gratings, and steel supports not part of the primary structure.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '05 50 00'; description matches: misc metals, miscellaneous fabrications, ladders, railings

nodes.name: Curtain Wall
nodes.desc: Aluminum-framed glass curtain wall systems — unitized or stick-built. High RFI and coordination issue activity (MEP penetrations, structural anchors). Major submittal packages. Trades: glaziers, curtain wall installers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '08 44 00'; description matches: curtain wall, glass curtain wall, structural glazing

nodes.name: Windows
nodes.desc: Punched window units in opaque wall systems. Product submittals drive procurement timeline. Trades: glaziers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '08 50 00'; description matches: windows, glazing, fenestration

nodes.name: Exterior Doors
nodes.desc: Entrance doors, storefronts, revolving doors, overhead doors, and service doors. Door hardware drives complexity. Trades: glaziers (storefronts), carpenters (hollow metal). Doors are one of the highest punch item categories.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '08 10 00'; description matches: exterior doors, hollow metal doors, storefront doors

nodes.name: Masonry Veneer
nodes.desc: All unit masonry — brick, block, and stone. Sub-specialties: bricklayers (face brick, thin brick veneer), block layers (CMU walls, backup walls, elevator shafts), and stone masons (natural and manufactured stone for exterior veneer and interior lobbies). Weather-sensitive — production depends on temperature and moisture. Measured in SF of wall face per day.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '04 20 00'; description matches: masonry veneer, brick veneer, stone veneer, CMU veneer

nodes.name: Precast / Metal Panels
nodes.desc: Architectural precast concrete, insulated metal panels (IMP), ACM panels, and terra cotta. Panel layout coordination with structure and MEP is critical. Trades: ironworkers (erection), specialty installers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '07 42 00 / 03 45 00'; description matches: metal panels, precast panels, architectural precast, MCM panels

nodes.name: Air & Moisture Barrier
nodes.desc: Fluid-applied or sheet membrane air/vapor barriers on exterior sheathing. Performance-critical — failures here cause long-term moisture damage. Third-party inspection is common. Trades: specialty applicators.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '07 25 00'; description matches: air barrier, moisture barrier, weather barrier

nodes.name: Exterior Insulation
nodes.desc: Continuous insulation (CI) on exterior walls — mineral wool, rigid foam, or spray foam. Energy code compliance driver. Trades: specialty installers, general laborers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '07 21 00'; description matches: exterior insulation, EIFS, continuous insulation

nodes.name: Roof Membrane
nodes.desc: Single-ply (TPO, PVC, EPDM), built-up, or modified bitumen roofing membranes. Warranty is a key deliverable — manufacturer requirements drive installation quality. Trades: roofers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '07 50 00'; description matches: roof membrane, TPO, EPDM, modified bitumen, built-up roofing

nodes.name: Roof Insulation
nodes.desc: Rigid insulation and tapered systems for drainage, with cover boards. Tapered layout is a design coordination item. Trades: roofers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '07 22 00'; description matches: roof insulation, rigid insulation, polyiso, tapered insulation

nodes.name: Flashing
nodes.desc: Roof edge metal, copings, counterflashing, reglets, gutters, and downspouts. Flashing failures are a top source of roof leaks. Trades: roofers, sheet metal workers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '07 62 00'; description matches: flashing, counter flashing, edge metal, copings

nodes.name: Roof Drainage
nodes.desc: Interior roof drains, scuppers, overflow drains, and conductor piping. Cross-trade coordination — roofer installs drain bodies, plumber runs pipe. Design drives structural loads (ponding).
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '07 71 00 / 22 14 00'; description matches: roof drainage, scupper, roof drain, downspout

nodes.name: Skylights
nodes.desc: Unit skylights, smoke vents, and roof access hatches. Small scope but high defect rate per unit — curb flashing is a common failure point. Trades: glaziers, specialty installers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '08 60 00'; description matches: skylights, roof glazing, smoke vents

nodes.name: Ductwork
nodes.desc: Supply, return, and exhaust ductwork — sheet metal, flex, and fittings. Routing coordination with other MEP systems drives schedule. Trades: HVAC/sheet metal workers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '23 30 00'; description matches: ductwork, sheet metal, HVAC duct, ducting

nodes.name: HVAC Piping
nodes.desc: Chilled water, hot water, condenser water, and refrigerant piping with insulation. Pipe sizing and routing are key RFI topics. Trades: pipefitters.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '23 20 00'; description matches: HVAC piping, hydronic piping, refrigerant piping

nodes.name: Air Handling Equip
nodes.desc: AHUs, RTUs, fan coil units, VAV boxes, and exhaust fans. Major submittals — equipment delivery is a key scheduling milestone. Trades: HVAC mechanics.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '23 70 00'; description matches: air handling unit, AHU, RTU, rooftop unit

nodes.name: Heating Equipment
nodes.desc: Boilers, unit heaters, radiant heating, and heat exchangers. Seasonal dependency — heating system must be operational for winter work. Trades: HVAC mechanics.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '23 50 00 / 23 80 00'; description matches: boilers, furnaces, heating equipment, hot water heaters

nodes.name: Cooling Equipment
nodes.desc: Chillers, cooling towers, condensing units, and split systems. Heaviest and most expensive MEP equipment — rigging and crane logistics are critical. Trades: HVAC mechanics.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '23 60 00 / 23 80 00'; description matches: chillers, cooling tower, cooling equipment, AC units, condensers

nodes.name: Duct Insulation
nodes.desc: Thermal and acoustic insulation on ductwork. Required by energy code on exposed supply and return duct. Trades: insulators.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '23 07 00'; description matches: duct insulation, HVAC insulation

nodes.name: HVAC Controls
nodes.desc: DDC controls, thermostats, sensors, damper actuators, and building automation system (BAS) programming. Controls/commissioning generates heavy RFI and punch item activity. Trades: controls technicians, HVAC mechanics.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '23 09 00 / 25 30 00'; description matches: HVAC controls, BAS controls, DDC, thermostats

nodes.name: TAB
nodes.desc: Testing, Adjusting, and Balancing technician who measures and adjusts air and water flow rates to design values — air system balancing, hydronic balancing, sound and vibration measurement. TAB firm independence from MEP contractor is important for objectivity. Active during Commissioning phase. Decision authority: Low — measures and adjusts to design values; reports deficiencies; does not redesign systems.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '23 05 93'; description matches: TAB, testing adjusting balancing, air balancing

nodes.name: Domestic Water
nodes.desc: Hot and cold water distribution piping, valves, hangers, and insulation. Leak testing is a key milestone. Trades: plumbers/pipefitters.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '22 11 00'; description matches: domestic water, potable water, hot/cold water lines

nodes.name: Waste & Vent
nodes.desc: DWV piping, cleanouts, floor drains, and traps. Gravity-driven — slope is critical and generates RFIs for routing in tight ceilings. Trades: plumbers/pipefitters.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '22 13 00'; description matches: waste, vent, sanitary piping, DWV

nodes.name: Interior Storm Drain
nodes.desc: Interior roof drain leaders, area drains, and conductor piping. Connects roof drainage to underground storm system. Trades: plumbers/pipefitters.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '22 14 00'; description matches: interior storm drain, roof drain piping

nodes.name: Gas Piping
nodes.desc: Interior natural gas distribution to equipment — boilers, water heaters, kitchen equipment. Requires pressure testing and inspection. Trades: plumbers/pipefitters.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '22 11 23'; description matches: gas piping, natural gas, propane piping

nodes.name: Plumbing Fixtures
nodes.desc: Toilets, urinals, sinks, faucets, drinking fountains, and floor drains. High submittal activity (product approvals) and very high punch item rates — plumbing fixtures are a top punch category. Trades: plumbers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '22 40 00'; description matches: plumbing fixtures, water closets, lavatories, sinks

nodes.name: Water Heaters
nodes.desc: Domestic water heating — tanks, tankless, and heat pump water heaters. Small scope but affects hot water distribution design. Trades: plumbers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '22 30 00'; description matches: water heaters, hot water generators, tankless heaters

nodes.name: Pipe Insulation
nodes.desc: Thermal insulation on plumbing piping. Required by energy code on hot water and chilled supply lines. Trades: insulators.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '22 07 00'; description matches: pipe insulation, plumbing insulation

nodes.name: Power Distribution
nodes.desc: Main switchgear, distribution panels, transformers, bus duct, and feeders. Major submittals with long lead times — procurement timing is critical. Trades: electricians.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '26 20 00'; description matches: power distribution, switchgear, panelboards, transformers

nodes.name: Branch Wiring
nodes.desc: Outlets, switches, junction boxes, home runs, and branch circuits. Highest-volume electrical work. Punch items for cover plates and device alignment are top closeout issues. Trades: electricians.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '26 27 00'; description matches: branch wiring, branch circuit, receptacles wiring

nodes.name: Conduit & Raceways
nodes.desc: EMT, rigid, PVC conduit, cable tray, and wireways. Routing coordination with other MEP systems is a major schedule driver. Trades: electricians.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '26 05 33'; description matches: conduit, raceway, EMT, rigid conduit

nodes.name: Lighting
nodes.desc: All light fixtures and controls — interior, exterior, emergency, and controls. Interior fixtures (troffers, downlights, pendants, linear, decorative), exterior fixtures (site, facade, parking, landscape lighting), emergency and exit (emergency battery units, exit signs, egress lighting — code-required), and lighting controls (dimmers, occupancy sensors, daylight harvesting, relay panels). Lighting submittals are among the highest volume on any project — every fixture type requires approval.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '26 50 00'; description matches: lighting, light fixtures, luminaires

nodes.name: Emergency Power
nodes.desc: Emergency and standby power systems including generators, transfer switches, UPS systems, and associated distribution for life safety and critical loads.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '26 30 00'; description matches: emergency power, generator, UPS, ATS

nodes.name: Grounding
nodes.desc: Building grounding system, lightning rods, and bonding. Code-required; tested during construction. Small scope. Trades: electricians.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '26 05 26'; description matches: grounding, bonding, ground rods

nodes.name: Sprinkler Mains
nodes.desc: Fire sprinkler distribution piping — mains, cross-mains, and branch lines with seismic bracing. Head layout coordination with ceilings and MEP is a top coordination issue. Trades: sprinkler fitters.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '21 11 00'; description matches: sprinkler main, fire main, fire suppression piping

nodes.name: Sprinkler Heads
nodes.desc: Pendant, upright, sidewall, and concealed sprinkler heads. Very high punch item rate — concealed head cover plates are a top closeout item. Trades: sprinkler fitters.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '21 13 00'; description matches: sprinkler head, sprinkler, fire suppression head

nodes.name: Standpipes
nodes.desc: Standpipe risers, hose valves, and fire department connections (FDC). Required for buildings above certain heights. Flow testing is a code requirement. Trades: sprinkler fitters.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '21 12 00'; description matches: standpipe, fire department connection

nodes.name: Fire Pump
nodes.desc: Fire pump, jockey pump, controller, and test header. Required when municipal water pressure is insufficient. Acceptance testing is code-required. Trades: sprinkler fitters, electricians.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '21 30 00'; description matches: fire pump, jockey pump, fire pump skid

nodes.name: Fire Alarm
nodes.desc: Install visible fire alarm devices — smoke/heat detectors, pull stations, horn/strobes, duct detectors, device addressing, panel programming. Device placement must match approved fire alarm drawings. Gate: devices complete — ready for acceptance testing. Duration: 1-2%.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '28 31 00'; description matches: fire alarm, FA panel, smoke detector, pull station

nodes.name: Data / Telecom
nodes.desc: Cat6/fiber optic cabling, patch panels, racks, and telecom rooms. Pathway coordination with electrical. Trades: low-voltage technicians.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '27 10 00 / 27 20 00'; description matches: data, telecom, structured cabling, voice/data

nodes.name: Security Systems
nodes.desc: CCTV cameras, access control (card readers, controllers), and intrusion detection. Integrates with door hardware — coordination signal. Trades: low-voltage technicians.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '28 20 00'; description matches: security, access control, video surveillance, intrusion

nodes.name: Audio / Visual
nodes.desc: AV systems — displays, speakers, distributed audio, and conference room systems. Often owner-furnished / contractor-installed (OFCI). Trades: low-voltage technicians.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '27 40 00'; description matches: audio visual, AV, sound system

nodes.name: Building Automation
nodes.desc: Building Automation System (BAS/BMS) — centralized control system that monitors and manages HVAC, lighting, fire/life safety, security, and other building systems. Integrates sensors, controllers, and actuators via communication protocols (BACnet, Modbus, LonWorks). Active from MEP rough-in through operations. Key integration point for commissioning and ongoing facility management.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '25 30 00'; description matches: BAS, building automation, integrated automation

nodes.name: Specialty Systems
nodes.desc: Nurse call, intercom, paging, distributed antenna (DAS), and clock systems. Project-type dependent — nurse call for healthcare, DAS for large buildings. Trades: low-voltage technicians.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '13 00 00'; description matches: specialty systems, special construction, special equipment

nodes.name: Metal Stud Framing
nodes.desc: Interior metal stud partitions, soffits, and bulkhead framing with blocking. Layout accuracy drives downstream finish quality. Trades: drywall hangers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '09 22 00'; description matches: metal studs, light gauge framing, drywall framing

nodes.name: Drywall
nodes.desc: Hang and finish gypsum board — drywall hanging, taping and finishing, sanding, specialty boards (moisture/abuse/fire-rated), patching around MEP. Above-ceiling inspection must happen BEFORE drywall closes the ceiling. Gate: drywall complete — walls ready for finishes. Duration: 3-5%.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '09 29 00'; description matches: drywall, gypsum board, GWB

nodes.name: Interior Doors
nodes.desc: Interior hollow metal and wood doors, frames, and finish hardware (hinges, closers, locks, pulls). Hardware sets are complex submittals. Doors/hardware is consistently a top 3 punch item category. Trades: carpenters.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '08 14 00'; description matches: interior doors, wood doors

nodes.name: Acoustic Ceilings
nodes.desc: Suspended acoustic ceiling grid and tile systems. Layout coordination with lighting, sprinklers, and diffusers. Trades: ceiling installers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '09 51 00'; description matches: acoustic ceiling, ACT, suspended ceiling

nodes.name: Drywall Ceilings
nodes.desc: Hang and finish gypsum board — drywall hanging, taping and finishing, sanding, specialty boards (moisture/abuse/fire-rated), patching around MEP. Above-ceiling inspection must happen BEFORE drywall closes the ceiling. Gate: drywall complete — walls ready for finishes. Duration: 3-5%.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '09 29 00'; description matches: drywall ceiling, gyp ceiling

nodes.name: Paint & Coatings
nodes.desc: All painting and coating products — interior paint, exterior coatings, specialty coatings, primers, and wall coverings. Interior paint (latex, acrylic for walls, ceilings, trim), exterior paint and coatings (elastomeric, acrylic, high-performance), specialty coatings (epoxy, urethane, anti-graffiti, antimicrobial), primers and sealers (metal primer, wood sealer, block filler), and wall coverings (vinyl, fabric, FRP panels). Paint schedules are a standard submittal.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '09 91 00'; description matches: paint, painting, coatings

nodes.name: Wallcovering
nodes.desc: Vinyl wallcovering, specialty wall finishes, and wall protection (corner guards, crash rails). Common in healthcare and hospitality. Trades: painters, specialty installers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '09 72 00'; description matches: wallcovering, vinyl wall covering

nodes.name: Floor Tile
nodes.desc: Ceramic, porcelain, and natural stone tile for floors and walls — including setting materials and grout. Cracked tiles, grout, and lippage are common punch items. Trades: tile setters.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '09 30 00'; description matches: tile, ceramic tile, porcelain tile, mosaic

nodes.name: Carpet & Resilient
nodes.desc: Carpet (broadloom, tile), VCT, LVT, rubber, and sheet vinyl with resilient base. Seams, transitions, and base are frequent closeout items. Trades: floor installers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '09 65 00 / 09 68 00'; description matches: carpet, resilient flooring, VCT, LVT

nodes.name: Specialty Flooring
nodes.desc: Polished concrete, epoxy, terrazzo, and resinous flooring. Project-type dependent — common in industrial, healthcare, and education. Trades: specialty installers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '09 60 00'; description matches: specialty flooring, sports floor, raised floor

nodes.name: Casework
nodes.desc: Cabinets, countertops, shelving, built-in furniture, and wood trim (base/crown molding). High punch item rate — scratches, chips, alignment, and door adjustment. Trades: carpenters.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '06 41 00 / 12 30 00'; description matches: casework, millwork, cabinetry

nodes.name: Countertops
nodes.desc: Solid surface, stone, quartz, and plastic laminate countertops. Templated after casework installation — template-to-install cycle is a schedule constraint. Trades: specialty installers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '12 36 00'; description matches: countertops, solid surface, quartz, granite

nodes.name: Toilet Accessories
nodes.desc: Toilet partitions, accessories (paper holders, grab bars, soap dispensers), mirrors, and signage. High punch item rate — installation alignment, missing items, and damage. Trades: carpenters, general laborers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '10 28 00'; description matches: toilet accessories, partitions, grab bars

nodes.name: Signage
nodes.desc: Room signs, directional signs, code-required signs, and ADA signage. Late-project scope — often last items installed before occupancy. Trades: specialty installers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '10 14 00'; description matches: signage, signs, wayfinding

nodes.name: Elevator Car
nodes.desc: Passenger, service, and freight elevator cars — hoistway doors, cab finishes, and controls. Elevator installation is a major scheduling milestone. Long lead time item. Trades: elevator mechanics.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '14 24 00'; description matches: elevator car, cab, elevator interior

nodes.name: Elevator Machinery
nodes.desc: Traction machines, hydraulic units, motor controllers, and dispatching systems. Acceptance testing/inspection by authority having jurisdiction (AHJ) is required. Trades: elevator mechanics.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '14 21 00'; description matches: elevator machinery, hoist, motor, controller

nodes.name: Paving
nodes.desc: Asphalt and concrete paving for parking lots, access roads, and loading areas. Temperature-sensitive installation (asphalt). Trades: equipment operators, general laborers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '32 12 00 / 32 13 00'; description matches: paving, asphalt paving, concrete paving

nodes.name: Sidewalks
nodes.desc: Concrete sidewalks, curbs, gutters, ADA ramps, and pavers. ADA compliance is a common observation and punch item trigger. Trades: concrete workers.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '32 13 13'; description matches: sidewalks, walks, pedestrian paving

nodes.name: Landscaping
nodes.desc: Trees, shrubs, ground cover, turf, and irrigation systems. Usually one of the last exterior scopes. Plant survival warranty (typically 1 year) extends beyond project completion. Trades: landscape contractors.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '32 90 00'; description matches: landscape, landscaping, planting, irrigation

nodes.name: Site Lighting
nodes.desc: Parking lot and pathway lighting — poles, bollard lights, and site electrical. Coordinates with site electrical underground. Trades: electricians.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '33 71 00'; description matches: site lighting, exterior lighting, parking lot lighting

nodes.name: Fencing
nodes.desc: Chain link, ornamental, and security fencing with gates and barriers. Small scope on most projects. Trades: fence contractors.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division ~= '32 31 00'; description matches: fencing, fence, gate

nodes.name: Reinforcing Element
nodes.desc: Generic parent for steel reinforcement embedded in concrete. IFC: IfcReinforcingElement. Provides tensile strength to concrete (which is weak in tension).
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat division 03 20 00 (Concrete Reinforcing). Parent concept.

nodes.name: Reinforcing Bar (Rebar)
nodes.desc: Deformed steel bar embedded in concrete. IFC: IfcReinforcingBar. Sized #3 through #18 in US conventions. Quantities tracked by weight (lbs/tons) and length, drives reinforcement schedule.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat 03 21 00 (Reinforcing Steel). Filter cost code descriptions for 'rebar' / 'reinforcing bar' / '#3-#11'.

nodes.name: Reinforcing Mesh
nodes.desc: Welded wire fabric used in slabs, walls, pavements. IFC: IfcReinforcingMesh. Lighter alternative to bar reinforcement for distributed loading.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat 03 22 00 (Welded Wire Reinforcement). Filter descriptions for 'mesh' / 'WWF' / 'welded wire'.

nodes.name: Tendon
nodes.desc: Pre- or post-tensioned steel cable in concrete — provides active compression. IFC: IfcTendon. Used in long-span beams, parking garages, post-tensioned slabs.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat 03 38 00 (Post-Tensioned Concrete). Filter descriptions for 'tendon' / 'PT' / 'post-tensioning'.

nodes.name: Tendon Anchor
nodes.desc: End fitting that locks tendon stress into the concrete. IFC: IfcTendonAnchor. Critical durability and inspection point.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat 03 38 00 + description contains 'anchor' / 'anchorage' / 'live end' / 'dead end'.

nodes.name: Tendon Conduit
nodes.desc: Sheathing through which a tendon runs — protects from corrosion and allows post-tensioning. IFC: IfcTendonConduit.
nodes.derivation: finance.cost_codes WHERE MasterFormat 03 38 00 + description contains 'conduit' / 'duct' / 'sheathing'.
